# 🧠 Model Guide — AI-Business Platform

> Complete reference for all AI models used in the platform.  
> **Hardware**: RTX 5070 (12GB VRAM) · 64GB RAM · Windows  
> **Last Updated**: 2026-06-14

---

## 1. Model Inventory

| Model | Type | Size (Disk) | VRAM Usage | Location | Purpose |
|-------|------|-------------|------------|----------|---------|
| SDXL Base 1.0 | Checkpoint | ~7 GB | ~6 GB | `D:\ComfyUI\ComfyUI\models\checkpoints\` | Coloring page generation (with LoRA) |
| Flux.1 Dev FP8 | Diffusion Model | ~17 GB | ~10 GB | `D:\ComfyUI\ComfyUI\models\diffusion_models\` | Wall art generation |
| CLIP-L | Text Encoder | 235 MB | ~0.5 GB | `D:\ComfyUI\ComfyUI\models\text_encoders\` | Text encoding for Flux |
| T5-XXL FP8 | Text Encoder | 4.9 GB | ~5 GB | `D:\ComfyUI\ComfyUI\models\text_encoders\` | Advanced text encoding for Flux |
| ColoringBook.Redmond | LoRA | ~200 MB | ~0.2 GB | `D:\ComfyUI\ComfyUI\models\loras\` | Line art coloring book style |
| 4x-UltraSharp | Upscaler | 64 MB | ~0.5 GB | `D:\ComfyUI\ComfyUI\models\upscale_models\` | 4× image upscaling for print |
| Flux VAE (ae.sft) | VAE | 320 MB | ~0.3 GB | `D:\ComfyUI\ComfyUI\models\vae\` | Latent-to-pixel decoding |
| Qwen 2.5 14B | LLM | ~9 GB | ~8 GB* | Managed by Ollama | Product listings & SEO copy |

> \* Ollama auto-offloads to system RAM when GPU VRAM is in use by ComfyUI.

---

## 2. Image Generation Models

### SDXL Base 1.0 — Coloring Pages

| Property | Value |
|----------|-------|
| **Purpose** | Coloring book line art (paired with ColoringBook.Redmond LoRA) |
| **File** | `D:\ComfyUI\ComfyUI\models\checkpoints\sd_xl_base_1.0.safetensors` |
| **Size** | ~7 GB |
| **VRAM Usage** | ~6 GB |
| **Native Resolution** | 1024×1024 |
| **License** | CreativeML Open RAIL++-M (commercial OK ✅) |
| **Strengths** | Negative prompt support, huge LoRA ecosystem, fast generation (~8s/image) |
| **Weaknesses** | Lower quality than Flux for photorealism |

**Recommended Generation Settings:**

| Parameter | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| Steps | 25 |
| CFG Scale | 7.0 |
| Sampler | `euler` |
| Scheduler | `normal` |
| Resolution | 1024×1024 |
| Negative Prompt | `color, shading, gradient, photorealistic, 3d, blurry, watermark` |

**Comparison: When to Use SDXL vs Flux**

| Criteria | SDXL + LoRA | Flux.1 Dev FP8 |
|----------|-------------|----------------|
| Use Case | Coloring pages (line art) | Wall art (full color) |
| Negative Prompts | ✅ Supported | ❌ Not supported |
| LoRA Support | ✅ Extensive | ⚠️ Limited (Flux LoRAs) |
| Generation Speed | ~8 sec/image | ~25 sec/image |
| VRAM Required | ~6 GB | ~10 GB |
| Quality | Great for stylized art | Best for photorealism |

---

### Flux.1 Dev FP8 — Wall Art

| Property | Value |
|----------|-------|
| **Purpose** | High-quality full-color wall art for Etsy |
| **File** | `D:\ComfyUI\ComfyUI\models\diffusion_models\flux1-dev-fp8.safetensors` |
| **Size** | ~17 GB |
| **VRAM Usage** | ~10 GB (FP8 quantized to fit in 12GB) |
| **Resolution** | 768×1152 (2:3), 1024×1024, or custom aspect ratios |

| **Strengths** | Photorealistic, artistic styles, follows complex prompts precisely |
| **Weaknesses** | No negative prompt, large VRAM footprint, slower generation |

**Recommended Generation Settings:**

| Parameter | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| Steps | 20 |
| Guidance | 3.5 |
| Sampler | `euler` |
| Scheduler | `simple` |
| Resolution | 768×1152 (portrait) or 1024×1024 (square) |

> **Note**: Flux uses "guidance" instead of "CFG scale". Values between 3.0–4.0 work best.

**Required Companion Models for Flux:**
- CLIP-L text encoder
- T5-XXL FP8 text encoder
- Flux VAE (`ae.safetensors`)

## 4. Upscaler

| Weight | Effect |

| 0.7–0.8 | **Sweet spot** — clean lines, minimal shading |
| 0.9–1.0 | Very strong line art, may lose fine detail |

**Prompt Template for Coloring Pages:**
```
ColoringBookAF, coloring book page, black and white line art,
clean outlines, {subject}, simple background, no shading,
no color, thick lines, children's coloring book style
```

**Recommended Temperature Settings:**

| Task | Temperature | Top-P |
|------|-------------|-------|
| SEO product descriptions | 0.3 | 0.9 |
| Creative marketing copy | 0.7 | 0.95 |
| Keyword generation | 0.2 | 0.8 |
| Etsy tags | 0.3 | 0.85 |

**Custom Modelfile (Optional):**
```dockerfile
FROM qwen2.5:14b
PARAMETER temperature 0.5
PARAMETER top_p 0.9
PARAMETER num_ctx 8192
SYSTEM "You are an expert e-commerce copywriter specializing in Amazon KDP and Etsy listings. Write compelling, SEO-optimized product descriptions."
```

Create with:
```bash
ollama create kdp-writer -f Modelfile
```

**Python Integration:**
```python
import requests

def generate_listing(prompt, temperature=0.5):
    response = requests.post(
        "http://127.0.0.1:11434/api/generate",
        json={
            "model": "qwen2.5:14b",
            "prompt": prompt,
            "stream": False,
            "options": {
                "temperature": temperature,
                "top_p": 0.9,
                "num_ctx": 8192
            }
        }
    )
    return response.json()["response"]
```

---

## 7. LoRA Training Guide

### Training Custom LoRAs on RTX 5070 (12GB VRAM)

Training is **possible but slow**. Your 64GB system RAM is a significant advantage.

**Tool**: [Kohya_ss (sd-scripts)](https://github.com/kohya-ss/sd-scripts)

#### Required Memory Optimization Settings

| Parameter | Value | Why |
|-----------|-------|-----|
| **Fused Backward Pass** | `--fused_backward_pass` | Reduces peak VRAM by fusing gradient computation |
| **Block Swapping** | `--blocks_to_swap 20` | Swaps transformer blocks to CPU RAM (20+ recommended) |
| **FP8 Base Model** | `--fp8_base` | Quantizes base model to FP8 during training |
| **Gradient Checkpointing** | `--gradient_checkpointing` | Trades compute for memory |
| **Batch Size** | `1` | Minimum to fit in VRAM |
| **Optimizer** | `Adafactor` or `8-bit Adam` | Lower memory optimizers |
| **Mixed Precision** | `bf16` | RTX 5070 supports BF16 natively |
| **Cache Latents** | `--cache_latents` | Pre-compute and cache VAE latents |
| **Cache Text Encoders** | `--cache_text_encoder_outputs` | Pre-compute text embeddings |

#### Training Parameters

| Parameter | SDXL LoRA | Flux LoRA |
|-----------|-----------|-----------|
| **Learning Rate** | 1e-4 | 5e-5 |
| **LR Scheduler** | cosine | cosine |
| **Steps** | 1,500–3,000 | 1,000–2,000 |
| **Rank** | 32 | 16 |
| **Alpha** | 16 | 8 |
| **Dataset Size** | 20–50 images | 20–50 images |
| **Resolution** | 1024 | 1024 |
| **Time Estimate** | 1–2 hours | 2–4 hours |

#### Training Workflow

1. **Prepare Dataset**: Collect 20–50 high-quality images that represent your target style
2. **Caption Images**: Use auto-captioning (BLIP/WD14) or write manual captions
3. **Organize**: Place images + captions in `D:\AI-Business\training\{dataset_name}\`
4. **Configure Kohya_ss**: Load the 12GB VRAM preset with all optimizations above
5. **Train**: Monitor loss curve — should decrease and stabilize
6. **Test**: Load the LoRA in ComfyUI, generate test images at various weights (0.5–1.0)
7. **Iterate**: Adjust dataset, captions, or parameters if quality isn't sufficient
8. **Deploy**: Copy final `.safetensors` to `D:\ComfyUI\ComfyUI\models\loras\`

#### Sample Kohya_ss Command (SDXL LoRA)
```bash
accelerate launch train_network.py \
  --pretrained_model_name_or_path="D:\ComfyUI\ComfyUI\models\checkpoints\sd_xl_base_1.0.safetensors" \
  --train_data_dir="D:\AI-Business\training\my_style" \
  --output_dir="D:\AI-Business\training\output" \
  --output_name="my_custom_lora" \
  --network_module=networks.lora \
  --network_dim=32 \
  --network_alpha=16 \
  --resolution=1024 \
  --train_batch_size=1 \
  --max_train_steps=2000 \
  --learning_rate=1e-4 \
  --lr_scheduler=cosine \
  --mixed_precision=bf16 \
  --gradient_checkpointing \
  --fp8_base \
  --cache_latents \
  --cache_text_encoder_outputs \
  --optimizer_type=Adafactor \
  --save_every_n_steps=500
```

---

## 8. How to Swap / Update Models

### Replacing a Checkpoint

1. **Download** the new `.safetensors` file
2. **Place** it in the appropriate directory:
   - Checkpoints → `D:\ComfyUI\ComfyUI\models\checkpoints\`
   - Diffusion models → `D:\ComfyUI\ComfyUI\models\diffusion_models\`
3. **Update workflow JSON**: Open the relevant workflow in `D:\AI-Business\workflows\` and change the model filename:
   ```diff
   - "ckpt_name": "sd_xl_base_1.0.safetensors"
   + "ckpt_name": "new_model_name.safetensors"
   ```
4. **Refresh ComfyUI**: Click the refresh button in the UI, or restart ComfyUI
5. **Test**: Generate a single test image to verify the new model works
6. **Update config**: Edit `D:\AI-Business\config\model_configs.json`:
   ```json
   {
     "coloring_page_model": "new_model_name.safetensors",
     "wall_art_model": "flux1-dev-fp8.safetensors"
   }
   ```

### Replacing a LoRA

1. Download the new LoRA `.safetensors`
2. Place in `D:\ComfyUI\ComfyUI\models\loras\`
3. Update workflow JSON — find the LoRA loader node:
   ```diff
   - "lora_name": "ColoringBookRedmond.safetensors"
   + "lora_name": "NewLoRA.safetensors"
   ```
4. **Update trigger words** in prompt templates if the new LoRA uses different triggers
5. Test at weights 0.5, 0.7, 0.8, 1.0 to find the sweet spot

### Updating Ollama Model

```bash
# Pull a newer/different model
ollama pull qwen2.5:14b

# Or switch to a different model entirely
ollama pull llama3.1:8b

# Update the model name in scripts
# Edit D:\AI-Business\config\model_configs.json
```

### Post-Swap Checklist

- [ ] New model file exists in correct directory
- [ ] Workflow JSON updated with new filename
- [ ] Config file (`model_configs.json`) updated
- [ ] Test generation produces expected output
- [ ] VRAM usage is within 12GB limit
- [ ] No errors in `D:\AI-Business\logs\comfyui_client.log`

---

## 9. Recommended LoRAs from Civitai

### Where to Search

Browse [Civitai.com](https://civitai.com) → Filter by:
- **Model Type**: LoRA
- **Base Model**: SDXL 1.0 (for coloring pages) or Flux.1 Dev (for wall art)
- **Sort**: Most Downloaded or Highest Rated

### Recommended Categories

| Category | Search Terms | Use Case | Recommended Weight |
|----------|-------------|----------|-------------------|
| **Coloring Book** | `coloring book`, `line art`, `outline` | KDP coloring books | 0.7–0.9 |
| **Watercolor** | `watercolor`, `aquarelle`, `paint` | Etsy wall art | 0.6–0.8 |
| **Line Art** | `line art`, `ink drawing`, `pen sketch` | Detailed coloring pages | 0.7–0.9 |
| **Minimalist** | `minimalist`, `simple`, `clean` | Modern wall art | 0.5–0.7 |
| **Vintage** | `vintage`, `retro`, `antique` | Themed wall art | 0.6–0.8 |
| **Botanical** | `botanical`, `plant illustration` | Nature coloring/art | 0.6–0.8 |
| **Mandala** | `mandala`, `geometric`, `symmetrical` | Meditation coloring | 0.7–0.9 |

### LoRA Evaluation Criteria

When selecting a LoRA from Civitai, check:

1. **Downloads & Ratings**: Prefer 1000+ downloads, 4.5+ stars
2. **Sample Images**: Do the examples match your quality standard?
3. **Base Model Compatibility**: Must match your checkpoint (SDXL 1.0 or Flux)
4. **File Size**: Typically 50–300 MB is normal. Avoid very large (>500 MB) or very small (<10 MB)
5. **Trigger Words**: Note what trigger words are required
6. **License**: Check if commercial use is allowed
7. **Version**: Use the latest version available

---

## 10. VRAM Management

### 12GB VRAM Budget

Your RTX 5070 has 12GB VRAM. Here's how different pipelines use it:

| Pipeline | Components Loaded | Total VRAM | Fits? |
|----------|-------------------|------------|-------|
| SDXL + LoRA (coloring) | SDXL (~6 GB) + LoRA (~0.2 GB) + VAE (~0.3 GB) | ~6.5 GB | ✅ Easy |
| Flux (wall art) | Flux FP8 (~10 GB) + CLIP-L (~0.5 GB) + T5 FP8 (~5 GB) + VAE (~0.3 GB) | ~10 GB* | ✅ Tight |
| Upscaling | 4x-UltraSharp (~0.5 GB) + image buffer | ~2 GB | ✅ Easy |
| Ollama (Qwen 14B) | Full model in VRAM | ~8 GB | ✅ Solo |
| ComfyUI + Ollama simultaneously | Both compete for VRAM | >12 GB | ❌ No |

> \* ComfyUI loads/unloads text encoders dynamically to stay within 12 GB for Flux.

### ⚠️ Golden Rule: One Pipeline at a Time

**Never run ComfyUI image generation and Ollama simultaneously on the GPU.**

The orchestrator should:
1. Generate all images first (ComfyUI uses GPU)
2. Wait for ComfyUI to release VRAM
3. Then generate listings (Ollama uses GPU, or CPU if VRAM still occupied)

> Ollama automatically offloads to system RAM (64GB available) when VRAM is full, so it still works — just slower.

### ComfyUI Memory Management Flags

| Flag | Effect | When to Use |
|------|--------|-------------|
| `--lowvram` | Aggressively offloads to CPU RAM | If OOM errors are frequent |
| `--gpu-only` | Keeps everything on GPU | Only if you have VRAM headroom |
| `--fp8_e4m3fn-unet` | Forces FP8 for UNet | Reduces VRAM for large models |
| `--fp16-vae` | Uses FP16 for VAE | Saves ~0.2 GB |
| (default) | Auto memory management | **Recommended for most cases** |

Start ComfyUI with memory flags:
```bash
python main.py --listen 127.0.0.1 --port 8188 --lowvram
```

### Monitoring VRAM Usage

```powershell
# Quick check
nvidia-smi

# Continuous monitoring (updates every 2 seconds)
nvidia-smi -l 2

# Just VRAM numbers
nvidia-smi --query-gpu=memory.used,memory.total,memory.free --format=csv
```

### OOM Troubleshooting

| Symptom | Cause | Fix |
|---------|-------|-----|
| `CUDA out of memory` | Model too large | Add `--lowvram` flag to ComfyUI |
| OOM during upscaling | Image too large in VRAM | Upscale in tiles (ComfyUI has a tiled upscale node) |
| OOM with Flux | Text encoders + model exceed 12GB | Ensure FP8 versions are used; add `--lowvram` |
| Slow generation after OOM | Fragmented VRAM | Restart ComfyUI to clear VRAM |
| Ollama very slow | GPU VRAM occupied by ComfyUI | Wait for ComfyUI to finish, or accept CPU-speed inference |

## LoRA Fine-Tuning (Phase 3)

| **Training Time** | 2-4 hours per LoRA |
| **Dataset** | 20-50 curated images |
| **Output** | Custom LoRA .safetensors file |

### When to Train a Custom LoRA
- After you've sold 5+ books and know what style sells best
- When you want a consistent "brand" style across all books
- When existing LoRAs don't match your target aesthetic exactly

### Training Workflow
1. Collect 20-50 best coloring pages from your output
2. Caption each image (or use auto-captioning)
3. Configure Kohya_ss with 12GB presets
4. Train for 1000-2000 steps
5. Test the LoRA in ComfyUI
6. Iterate until quality matches target

3. In ComfyUI, add a "Load LoRA" node and select it

### Switch models in the orchestrator
Update `D:\AI-Business\knowledge\model_configs.json` with the new model name.

+## 7. LoRA Training Guide
+
+### Training Custom LoRAs on RTX 5070 (12GB VRAM)
+
+Training is **possible but slow**. Your 64GB system RAM is a significant advantage.
+
+**Tool**: [Kohya_ss (sd-scripts)](https://github.com/kohya-ss/sd-scripts)
+
+#### Required Memory Optimization Settings
+
+| Parameter | Value | Why |
+|-----------|-------|-----|
+| **Fused Backward Pass** | `--fused_backward_pass` | Reduces peak VRAM by fusing gradient computation |
+| **Block Swapping** | `--blocks_to_swap 20` | Swaps transformer blocks to CPU RAM (20+ recommended) |
+| **FP8 Base Model** | `--fp8_base` | Quantizes base model to FP8 during training |
+| **Gradient Checkpointing** | `--gradient_checkpointing` | Trades compute for memory |
+| **Batch Size** | `1` | Minimum to fit in VRAM |
+| **Optimizer** | `Adafactor` or `8-bit Adam` | Lower memory optimizers |
+| **Mixed Precision** | `bf16` | RTX 5070 supports BF16 natively |
+| **Cache Latents** | `--cache_latents` | Pre-compute and cache VAE latents |
+| **Cache Text Encoders** | `--cache_text_encoder_outputs` | Pre-compute text embeddings |
+
+#### Training Parameters
+
+| Parameter | SDXL LoRA | Flux LoRA |
+|-----------|-----------|-----------|
+| **Learning Rate** | 1e-4 | 5e-5 |
+| **LR Scheduler** | cosine | cosine |
+| **Steps** | 1,500–3,000 | 1,000–2,000 |
+| **Rank** | 32 | 16 |
+| **Alpha** | 16 | 8 |
+| **Dataset Size** | 20–50 images | 20–50 images |
+| **Resolution** | 1024 | 1024 |
+| **Time Estimate** | 1–2 hours | 2–4 hours |
+
+#### Training Workflow
+
+1. **Prepare Dataset**: Collect 20–50 high-quality images that represent your target style
+2. **Caption Images**: Use auto-captioning (BLIP/WD14) or write manual captions
+3. **Organize**: Place images + captions in `D:\AI-Business\training\{dataset_name}\`
+4. **Configure Kohya_ss**: Load the 12GB VRAM preset with all optimizations above
+5. **Train**: Monitor loss curve — should decrease and stabilize
+6. **Test**: Load the LoRA in ComfyUI, generate test images at various weights (0.5–1.0)
+7. **Iterate**: Adjust dataset, captions, or parameters if quality isn't sufficient
+8. **Deploy**: Copy final `.safetensors` to `D:\ComfyUI\ComfyUI\models\loras\`
+
+#### Sample Kohya_ss Command (SDXL LoRA)
+```bash
+accelerate launch train_network.py \
+  --pretrained_model_name_or_path="D:\ComfyUI\ComfyUI\models\checkpoints\sd_xl_base_1.0.safetensors" \
+  --train_data_dir="D:\AI-Business\training\my_style" \
+  --output_dir="D:\AI-Business\training\output" \
+  --output_name="my_custom_lora" \
+  --network_module=networks.lora \
+  --network_dim=32 \
+  --network_alpha=16 \
+  --resolution=1024 \
+  --train_batch_size=1 \
+  --max_train_steps=2000 \
+  --learning_rate=1e-4 \
+  --lr_scheduler=cosine \
+  --mixed_precision=bf16 \
+  --gradient_checkpointing \
+  --fp8_base \
+  --cache_latents \
+  --cache_text_encoder_outputs \
+  --optimizer_type=Adafactor \
+  --save_every_n_steps=500
+```

+## 10. VRAM Management
+
+### 12GB VRAM Budget
+
+Your RTX 5070 has 12GB VRAM. Here's how different pipelines use it:
+
+| Pipeline | Components Loaded | Total VRAM | Fits? |
+|----------|-------------------|------------|-------|
+| SDXL + LoRA (coloring) | SDXL (~6 GB) + LoRA (~0.2 GB) + VAE (~0.3 GB) | ~6.5 GB | ✅ Easy |
+| Flux (wall art) | Flux FP8 (~10 GB) + CLIP-L (~0.5 GB) + T5 FP8 (~5 GB) + VAE (~0.3 GB) | ~10 GB* | ✅ Tight |
+| Upscaling | 4x-UltraSharp (~0.5 GB) + image buffer | ~2 GB | ✅ Easy |
+| Ollama (Qwen 14B) | Full model in VRAM | ~8 GB | ✅ Solo |
+| ComfyUI + Ollama simultaneously | Both compete for VRAM | >12 GB | ❌ No |
+
+> \* ComfyUI loads/unloads text encoders dynamically to stay within 12 GB for Flux.
+
+### ⚠️ Golden Rule: One Pipeline at a Time
+
+**Never run ComfyUI image generation and Ollama simultaneously on the GPU.**
+
+The orchestrator should:
+1. Generate all images first (ComfyUI uses GPU)
+2. Wait for ComfyUI to release VRAM
+3. Then generate listings (Ollama uses GPU, or CPU if VRAM still occupied)
+
+> Ollama automatically offloads to system RAM (64GB available) when VRAM is full, so it still works — just slower.
+
+### ComfyUI Memory Management Flags
+
+| Flag | Effect | When to Use |
+|------|--------|-------------|
+| `--lowvram` | Aggressively offloads to CPU RAM | If OOM errors are frequent |
+| `--gpu-only` | Keeps everything on GPU | Only if you have VRAM headroom |
+| `--fp8_e4m3fn-unet` | Forces FP8 for UNet | Reduces VRAM for large models |
+| `--fp16-vae` | Uses FP16 for VAE | Saves ~0.2 GB |
+| (default) | Auto memory management | **Recommended for most cases** |
+
+Start ComfyUI with memory flags:
+```bash
+python main.py --listen 127.0.0.1 --port 8188 --lowvram
+```
+
+### Monitoring VRAM Usage
+
+```powershell
+# Quick check
+nvidia-smi
+
+# Continuous monitoring (updates every 2 seconds)
+nvidia-smi -l 2
+
+# Just VRAM numbers
+nvidia-smi --query-gpu=memory.used,memory.total,memory.free --format=csv
+```
+
+### OOM Troubleshooting
+
+| Symptom | Cause | Fix |
+|---------|-------|-----|
+| `CUDA out of memory` | Model too large | Add `--lowvram` flag to ComfyUI |
+| OOM during upscaling | Image too large in VRAM | Upscale in tiles (ComfyUI has a tiled upscale node) |
+| OOM with Flux | Text encoders + model exceed 12GB | Ensure FP8 versions are used; add `--lowvram` |
+| Slow generation after OOM | Fragmented VRAM | Restart ComfyUI to clear VRAM |
+| Ollama very slow | GPU VRAM occupied by ComfyUI | Wait for ComfyUI to finish, or accept CPU-speed inference |
